solution.label: Osmotic Retirement Mesh
solution.design_principles: Embrace volatility rather than fight it. Contributions flow via 'pressure gradients' not schedules. Individual inconsistency becomes collective stability. The system absorbs rather than demands.
solution.core_mechanism: Contributions flow automatically based on the differential between your current earnings and your rolling average—high pressure periods absorb into retirement, low pressure periods draw from it.
solution.how_it_works: Every participant joins a mesh of ~5000 workers. System tracks each person's 'financial pressure': current income / 24-month rolling average. When pressure >1.2, excess flows into mesh automatically (captured at payment source via API). When pressure <0.8, you can draw against accumulated credits. The mesh collectively smooths: your individual volatility is noise, but 5000 people's aggregate is signal. Benefits calculated as participation-years × mesh's average lifetime return, not individual contribution history. Implementation requires: (1) income verification via payment APIs, (2) pressure gradient algorithm, (3) mesh allocation protocol.
solution.what_is_new: Rejects the 'consistent contribution' assumption entirely. Your account balance FLUCTUATES during working years by design. Retirement savings become a two-way buffer, not a one-way accumulator.
solution.why_it_works: Law of large numbers makes mesh income stable even when individuals are chaotic. By letting contributions vary with capacity rather than demanding fixed amounts, compliance becomes automatic—you never face the impossible choice between eating today and saving for tomorrow.
solution.why_it_fails: Mesh selection creates adverse selection risk—high earners might cluster into elite meshes. The two-way flow enables gaming: people might artificially suppress income during 'draw' periods. Trust in the mesh requires unprecedented transparency, which conflicts with financial privacy norms.
solution.medium_term: Gig workers gain retirement access for first time. Traditional pensions begin offering 'osmotic' tiers. New financial identity layer emerges connecting payment flows across platforms.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement transforms from a binary cliff into a gradual tide. The mesh becomes a new social structure—people feel financial kinship with thousands of strangers whose volatility cushions their own. Income inequality persists but its terror diminishes: everyone belongs to something that absorbs their shocks.
